participants.name: Eli Heidenreich
participants.notes: Listed as FB by PFF. Combine invite who ran 4.44 40, 35.5" vertical, 120" broad jump, 16 bench reps at combine. At pro day, did not re-run 40 or re-test broad/bench; ran agility drills he skipped at combine (shuttle 4.03, cone 6.50, vertical 36.0). Pro day weight 202 lbs vs. 198 lbs at combine. Height 6-0, arms 29 1/4" per combine measurements. Projects as WR/RB hybrid.

participants.name: Landon Robinson
participants.notes: First-team All-American and AAC Defensive Player of the Year. Was not invited to NFL Combine. PFF listed height 5-11, weight 293 at pro day. 40-yard dash 4.87 with 1.65 10-yard split would have led all DTs at the combine per GBN Report. Bruce Feldman Freaks List No. 13: 700-lb squat, 465-lb bench press, 20.1 MPH top speed.

participants.name: Blake Horvath
participants.notes: Navy starting QB. Listed at 6-1 on school roster. Likely did throwing drills and positional work only. No measurable or drill results found in available sources. Will commission as a naval aviator upon graduation.

participants.name: Alex Tecza
participants.notes: Navy starting RB. 158 carries for 902 yards and 11 rushing TDs in 2025. Mt. Lebanon HS teammate of Heidenreich. No specific drill results reported in available sources. Will commission into the Marine Corps.

additional_notes: Navy kicked off the 2026 pro day season on March 5, 2026 in Annapolis, MD. Per multiple sources, 22-32 NFL teams sent scouts (A to Z Sports reported over two dozen, Yardbarker reported KC Chiefs among them, GBN Report stated all 32 teams had reps present). The participant list of 11 players was confirmed per Navy Football via A to Z Sports/Yardbarker. Detailed testing results were only publicly reported for the two primary draft prospects: Eli Heidenreich (combine invite who added agility drills at pro day) and Landon Robinson (combine snub who posted elite DT numbers). PFF sourced results from @EricGalko and @TonyPauline. Heidenreich's pro day weight of 202 was up from his 198-lb combine weight. Robinson's pro day weight of 293 was up from his roster/Freaks list weight of ~287-291. No individual drill results were found for the other 9 participants (Horvath, Tecza, Chatman, Duhart, Walker, Campbell, Pirris, McShan, Kirkwood) in publicly available reporting. The Pittsburgh Post-Gazette covered both Heidenreich and Tecza but the article was behind a paywall and full results could not be accessed. Indoor/outdoor status of the workout was not specified in available sources.
